id.description: This is the main subscription id. This will be needed to edit and delete the subscription.

model.description: This is the type of data that will be sent. you will need to reqest multiple subscriptions if you want more than one model. Addtional details available in guide at https://docs.ns-api.com/docs/event-subscriptions-1

post-url.description: This is the url the API will be posting data to. Should be in the format including https://. Can make a custom port if needed and the URL should have a valid SSL certificate as well. Http:// is supproted but not ideal for production.

subscription-geo-support.description: This allows any added configurations to specify a hostname. Default to "yes"

user-scope.description: This is the scope that should be enforced in combination with the reseller, domain and user fields. it will be set using the scope used to create the subscription.

reseller.description: This is the reseller that the subscription will filter data for. * will be used for all resellersm, but only valid with Super User scope on the request.

domain.description: This is the domain that the subscription will filter data for. * will be used for all domains, but only valid with Super User scope or Reseller scope if reseller is not *  on the request.

user.description: This is the user that the subscription will filter data for. * will be used for all users.

subscription-creation-datetime.description: This is a timestamp of creation of the event subscrtipion.

subscription-expires-datetime.description: This timestamp in the format of YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS will be with the subscription will expire. After it expires it will be removed after some amount of time. If left blank it will set the expires time to the expires time of the timed token used, if using a APIkey it will be 20 years from the creation date that it will set for the expires for the new subscription

preferred-server.description: This is the server hostname for the prefered server for this subscription, meaning if this server is up and online it will be the one sending the events. It wil fail back to this servre after 60s of stability.

current-active-server.description: This is the current server that is running this schedule. It will chagne only when a new server takes over and is a read only field.

status.description: This is the status of the schedule. A new schedule will get "pending" till it gets some data to send and once successfull it will go to "active". If any attempts get a error over a interval it will toggle this field to "error" and reset only once it gets a clean interval of posts.

error-count.description: This is the number of attempted post's that resulted in a error, either an inability to send to a server (network,dns, timeout, etc..) or a response code that is above a 302. This value can be "reset" by setting it to 0 in a update request, but only 0 is allowed value.

posts-count.description: This is the number of attempted post's in this schedule. This includes error and success attempts. This value can be "reset" by setting it to 0 in a update request, but only 0 is allowed value.

DomainName.description: This is the main organization name. This is used to link resource to its group/tenant/organization/enterprise. ~ and * can be used alone in special cases to mean My Domain (~) and All Domains (\*).

Scopes.description: Will link this resource with a User Scope granting it certain permisionf to the system for use in API and Portal.

TimeZone.description: This is the time zone that the user is primarily located in

domain.description: This is the domain that the token will be limited to.

name-first-name.description: This is the first name for the user

name-last-name.description: This is the last name for the user

login-username.description: This is the login for the user used to access the portals and other applications. The default will be [user]@[domain] if not set otherwise.  This value is required to be unique across the entire system so 409 will be returned if not unique.

email-address.description: This is the email adresses or list of email addresses sepertaed with a semi-colon.

user-scope.description: This is the scope that should be enforced in combination with the reseller, domain and user fields. it will be set using the scope used to create the subscription.

department.description: This is the depertment that user belongs to in the domain. Can be any string to group users, a department is not require to be setup before in order to set it for a user.

site.description: This is the site or location for this user. The site will group users that are at the same phyiscal location, but can be used for other grouping purposes as well. New users will inherit some settings from the site if the site was previously setup. If the site is not previously created then a new site will be created with the value given on new user create assuming the domain's max sites setting has remaining sites.

time-zone.description: The timezone which the meeting was created with

voicemail-login-pin.description: This is the numberic pin that would be used by the user validating their account when connecting into voice IVR to check voicemail.

privacy.description: This will enable IP checking. Default is no

dial-plan.description: This is the dial plan set for the user. Dial plans are used to control action and forward options that will be used when dialing out from the user.

dial-policy.description: This value is the dial policy/permission for this user whch will control the destinations this user can dial. Generally dial policys are preconfigured at system level and selected at user level.

status-message.description: This is is a value that is often set by the end user to show their current status, often a away message of some kind.

user-presence-status.description: This is a read only presence status for the user that will be calculated by the core system based on registration, answer rules and active call status.

directory-name-number-dtmf-mapping.description: This is a 3 digit numberic mapping that correlates to the first 3 digits of a user's first or last name for dial by name directory. Example Smith would be "764" translating the letters to numbers using the normal phone number mappings.

voicemail-user-control-enabled.description: This allows any added configurations to specify a hostname. Default to "yes"

phone-numbers-to-allow-enabled.description: This allows any added configurations to specify a hostname. Default to "yes"

phone-numbers-to-reject-enabled.description: This allows any added configurations to specify a hostname. Default to "yes"

call-screening-enabled.description: This allows any added configurations to specify a hostname. Default to "yes"

ring-no-answer-timeout-seconds.description: This time setting in seconds will controll how long a inbound call attempt will attempt to connect the call before stopping and attempting the next option which might be a forward setting in answer rules or voicemail if enabled.

language-token.description: This is the language that is set for the user to be used for portal viewing.

limits-max-data-storage-kilobytes.description: This is the maximum value in kilobytes that is allowed to be stored in the user's voicemail, audio greetings and fax storage.

limits-max-active-calls-total.description: This is the maximum number of active calls allowed for the user.

directory-annouce-in-dial-by-name-enable.description: This allows any added configurations to specify a hostname. Default to "yes"

directory-override-order-duplicate-dtmf-.description: This value can be used in cases where multiple users have the same value for directory-name-number-dtmf-mapping, if they are the same then use this setting to set the order low to high for playback in dial by name directory menu.

voicemail-greeting-index.description: This is the index of the greeting that is enabled currently for the user. Greeting 0 is the generic "[name] is not available" message will be played.

voicemail-enabled.description: This allows any added configurations to specify a hostname. Default to "yes"

voicemail-receive-broadcast-enabled.description: This allows any added configurations to specify a hostname. Default to "yes"

reject-anonymous-calls-enabled.description: This will enable IP checking. Default is no

voicemail-playback-announce-datetime-rec.description: This will enable IP checking. Default is no

voicemail-playback-announce-caller-id.description: This will enable IP checking. Default is no

voicemail-playback-sort-newest-to-oldest.description: This allows any added configurations to specify a hostname. Default to "yes"

email-send-alert-new-voicemail-cc-list-c.description: This list of user's which will be forward copies of new voicemails left on that user.

email-send-alert-new-voicemail-behavior.description: This is the behavior that the voicemail logic will take after a voicmail is left. The options include ability to send with attachements or hyperlinks.

enumDescriptions.briefattsave: Brief (no html) and move to saved.
enumDescriptions.briefatttrash: Brief (no html) and move to trash.

email-send-alert-new-voicemail-enabled.description: This will enable IP checking. Default is no

email-send-alert-new-missed-call-enabled.description: This will enable IP checking. Default is no

email-send-alert-data-storage-limit-reac.description: This will enable IP checking. Default is no

caller-id-number.description: This is the main caller id number for the user to be used for outbound calls outside of the domain.

caller-id-name.description: This is the main caller id name for the user to be used for outbound calls outside of the domain.

caller-id-number-emergency.description: This is the main caller id that will be used for emergency calls when placed by the user.

area-code.description: This is a area code for the user. I will be used when user dial's 7 digits to translate it to full 10 or 11 digit number.

directory-name-visible-in-list-enabled.description: This allows any added configurations to specify a hostname. Default to "yes"

service-code.description: Service codes are for internal use that allow user's to be tagged for billing and tracking purposes. Any service code starting with "system-" will be hidden by default in the portals and not count as regular users.

voicemail-transcription-enabled.description: Enable this setting and select a vendor if you wish to have voicemails transcripted after a voicemail is recorded.

emergency-address-id.description: Value will need to be an exact match to an address-id which links to a address for DLR (dynamic location routing) for emergency calling

call-recordings-hide-from-others-enabled.description: This will enable IP checking. Default is no

music-on-hold-randomized-enabled.description: This will enable IP checking. Default is no

music-on-hold-comfort-message-repeat-int.description: This interval will control how frequently the music on hold would be interupted and have messages played. Should be used when using music on hod and custom messages.

active-calls-total-current.description: This read only setting is the current active call count for this user.

account-status.description: This status is read only and is calcuated to suggest special states that can be relayed to the end user or manager views. Status's include when a user is new, the user has been reset or pasword needs to be set.

created-datetime.description: This datetime is when the user was created.

last-modified-datetime.description: This datetime is when the user was last modified.

domain.description: This is the domain that the token will be limited to.

reseller.description: This is the reseller that owns this domain. Must be a valid resller name on creation. If Reseller level scope you can use "~" to set your own reseller on create, Super User's will need to set this to a valid Reseller.

description.description: This is a description of the domain. This will be shown in many places to help show a more user friendly version of the domain to help identify it.

music-on-hold-enabled.description: Enabled will trigger music on hold for the domain and allow it to be customized.

music-on-ring-enabled.description: Enabled will trigger music on hold for the domain and allow it to be customized.

music-on-hold-randomized-enabled.description: Enabled will trigger music on hold for the domain and allow it to be customized.

domain-type.description: Allows a special domain type to be set for specific use cases. Currently Residenital and Hospitality are options which will limit and effect the users expierence.

dial-plan.description: This is the default dial plan that will be used for new users created in the domain. A dial plan is created by default on new domain creation with a name matching the domain.

dial-policy.description: This is the default dial policy (permission) that will be used for new users added ot the domain. Generally these are system wide policies and will not need to create one per domain.

email-send-from-address.description: This is the email source that will be used when sending out emails for this domain. This address should be configured properly through the email server so that emails are not blocked. In most cases this is recommended to be an address on the service providers or resellers hostname so DNS SPF records can be added on the hostname to prevent spam issues.  More info on SPF records available via https://www.cloudflare.com/learning/dns/dns-records/dns-spf-record/

single-sign-on-enabled.description: Enabled will trigger music on hold for the domain and allow it to be customized.

area-code.description: The default area code for the domain. This is used by default on 7 digit dials to extent the number to a valid 10 digit number.

caller-id-name.description: This is the main caller id name for the user to be used for outbound calls outside of the domain.

caller-id-number.description: This is the main caller id number for the user to be used for outbound calls outside of the domain.

caller-id-number-emergency.description: This is the main caller id that will be used for emergency calls when placed by the user.

limits-max-subcriber-resources-total.description: This is the maximum number of subscribers allowed for domain including the subscribers that would be created as part of resources like call queues and auto attendants. If looking to limit nornal users it would be better to use limits-max-users

limits-max-active-calls-total.description: This is the limit for the number of active calls allowed in the domain simultaniously. Note: this will count and limit intra domain calls.

limits-max-active-calls-offnet.description: This is the limit for the number of active calls allowed in the domain simultaniously that are connected to offnet or external numbers.

limits-max-call-queues.description: This is the maximum number of call queues that would be allowed to be created in this domain.

limits-max-auto-attendants.description: This is the maximum number of auto attendants that would be allowed to be created in this domain.

limits-max-conferences.description: This is the maximum number of conference bridges that would be allowed to be created in this domain.

limits-max-departments.description: This is the maximum number of departments that would be allowed to be created in this domain.

limits-max-users.description: This is the maximum number of regular users that would be allowed to be created in this domain.

limits-max-sites.description: This is the maximum number of sites that would be allowed to be created in this domain.

limits-max-fax-accounts.description: This is the maximum number of fax accounts that would be allowed to be created in this domain.

time-zone.description: The timezone which the meeting was created with

email-send-smtp-hostname.description: This is the SMTP server hostname. SMTP server configs are NOT required at domain level as they will inherit reseller or system level as needed.

email-send-smtp-port.description: This is the SMTP port for sending emails. SMTP server configs are NOT required at domain level as they will inherit reseller or system level as needed.

email-send-smtp-username.description: This is the username used in connecting to SMTP  server for sending email. SMTP server configs are NOT required at domain level as they will inherit reseller or system level as needed.

email-send-smtp-password.description: This is the password used in connecting to SMTP  server for sending email. SMTP server configs are NOT required at domain level as they will inherit reseller or system level as needed.

active-calls-total-current.description: This is the total active calls for the domain including on-net (intra domain) calls and calls across geo servers and calls linked to "guest" video sessions

active-calls-against-license-count.description: This is the total active calls for the domain including on-net (intra domain) calls but counted as the license would be enforced inlcuding ignoring geo call legs and guest video sessions.

active-calls-offnet-current.description: This is the total active calls for the domain only including calls that are connected to a remote (non intra domain) caller/calling party.

count-subcriber-resources-total.description: This is the total number of users plus all the system users as well for the entire domain.

emergency-address-id.description: Value will need to be an exact match to an address-id which links to a address for DLR (dynamic location routing) for emergency calling

voicemail-enabled.description: Enabled will trigger music on hold for the domain and allow it to be customized.

voicemail-transcription-enabled.description: Enable this setting and select a vendor if you wish to have voicemails transcripted after a voicemail is recorded.

language-token.description: This is the language that is set for the user to be used for portal viewing.

is-domain-locked.description: Enabled will trigger music on hold for the domain and allow it to be customized.

is-stir-enabled.description: Enabled will trigger music on hold for the domain and allow it to be customized.

is-ivr-forward-change-blocked.description: Enabled will trigger music on hold for the domain and allow it to be customized.

id.description: This is the ID of the message

timestamp.description: This is when the message was recieved or sent

type.description: Type of message to send. Examples: chat, chat-media, sms, mms, chat-name-change, chat-video-invite, chat-participant-add, chat-participant-remove, chat-participant-leave

domain.description: This is the domain that the token will be limited to.

direction.description: This is the direction of the message. Either term for outbound or orig for inbound

from-number.description: This is the SMS number the message came from. Is not set for chat messages

from-user-id.description: This is the User ID the message came from. Not set for offnet numbers

from-video-attendee-id.description: This is the video attendee id the message came from if sent within a video call

from-user-agent.description: This is the HTTP USER AGENT the message came from

dialed.description: This is the number that this message is sent to. This can be comma seperated list of numbers if more than one in a group MMS session. Or null for chat sessions

text.description: This is the text sent in the message

terminating-user-id.description: This is the receiving User ID of the message

terminating-number.description: This is the number that this message is sent to. This can be comma seperated list of numbers if more than one in a group MMS session. Or null for chat sessions

status.description: This is the status of the message

video-instance-id.description: This is the video instance ID of the message if the message was sent from a video session.

media-type.description: This is the media type of the message if applicable

media-size.description: This is the media size of the message if applicable

deleted-datetime.description: This is the deleted timestamp of the message if the message was deleted by a host in a video meeting

anyOf.description: This is when the message was recieved or sent

messagesession-reciever-hostname.description: This is the receiver hostname for the message

messagesession-id.description: This is the messagesession ID for this message

messagesession-participants.description: This is the comma seperated participants of the messagesession associated with this message

domain.description: This is the domain that the token will be limited to.

voicemail-reminder-phonecall-enabled.description: This will enable IP checking. Default is no

voicemail-reminder-sms-enabled.description: This will enable IP checking. Default is no

voicemail-reminder-email-enabled.description: This will enable IP checking. Default is no

voicemail-reminder-control-server.description: Current server managing the reminders for the user

domain.description: This is the domain that the token will be limited to.

messagesession-remote.description: This is the UserID (ie, 1234@portal) or the phonenumber of the recipient(s) of the messages. If more than one, will be comma seperated

messagesession-muted.description: This is whether or not the message session is muted or not. "yes" or "no"

messagesession-id.description: This is the identifier for the session

messagesession-sms-number.description: This is the SMS number used to send outbound. Will be null if not an SMS message session

messagesession-last-datetime.description: This is the last time the session had an inbound or outbound message

messagesession-start-datetime.description: This is the when the message session started

messagesession-last-message.description: This is the last message in the messagesession

messagesession-last-sender.description: This shows the UID or the phonenumber of who was the last message sender

messagesession-last-status.description: This is the last status of the messagesession. Will contain read/unread status

messagesession-last-email-reminder-datet.description: This is when the last email reminder was sent out notifiying the user of a missed message

messagesession-reciever-hostname.description: This is the hostname of the last received message

messagesession-queue-termination.description: This is which SMS queue the messagesession is assigned to

messagesession-queue-status.description: This is the queue status of the messagesession

messagesession-queue-session-id.description: This is the queue session id placed on the messagesession

messagesession-agent-start-datetime.description: This is when the messagesession was started by the agent

messagesession-last-type.description: This is the last message type of the messagesession

anyOf.description: Type of message to send. Examples: chat, chat-media, sms, mms, chat-name-change, chat-video-invite, chat-participant-add, chat-participant-remove, chat-participant-leave

messagesession-last-media-type.description: This is the last media type of the messagesession

messagesession-last-media-size.description: This is the size of the last media of the messagesession

messagesession-type.description: This is the type of messagesession

messagesession-name.description: This is the name of the messagesession. Names can be assigned to group chats

messagesession-participants.description: This is a comma seperated list of participants within a group chat or group sms messagesession

device-provisioning-username.description: This is the per device provisioning username used for accessing the config file.

device-provisioning-password.description: This is the per device provisioning password used for accessing the config file.

global-one-time-pass.description: This will enable IP checking. Default is no

index.description: The index of the file, Example "2" for greeting 2. In create (POST) requests index cab be omitted and the API will select a new index automatically.

script.description: This is a simple explanation of what the audio is and can include what is said in the audio file for explanation.

convert.description: Setting yes will allow the API to attempt to convert the file from accepted formats into the correct format for media playback

encoding.description: Used when providing base64 encoded data to give the content tupe of the file.

base64_file.description: This is a base64 encoded string formated without line breaks or spaces.

voice_language.description: This is the language that can be requested when using TTS feature. Leave of to have API lookup configs from the defaults or domain specific configs.

voice_id.description: This value will allow you to select the voice to be used for TTS applications. Value must be valid or will cause errors. leave off request to have the API lookup defaults from system and/or domain.

filename.description: This is the filename without and path or folder. Should end in .wav

ordinal-order.description: This is the index of the audio file.

file-duration-seconds.description: This value is the length of the file in seconds.

file-size-kilobytes.description: This value will be a formated filesize of the file in kilobytes

created-datetime.description: This is the timestamp of create time fo the file

file-access-url.description: This will be URL of the file for remote playback.

file-script-text.description: This is the script that was used for text to speech and also used to create a new file.

file-source.description: This is the source of the file, it will show how the file was created originally.

text-to-speech-voice-id.description: This is the voice id used when using text to speech.

text-to-speech-language.description: This is the voice language when using TTS.

recorded-datetime.description: This is the timestamp of record time fo the file

examples: Used by Vendor Xyz for Billing Automation
description.description: This is the main description/name for the API key that should describe its purpose for tracking and understanding its intended use.

key-id.description: This is the Key ID that will identify the key and allow for managment, audit tracking and monitoring.

key.description: This is the key that will need to be saved securly for use in accessing the API. It will only be shown once.

reseller.description: This is the reseller that will limit the API key to access the resellers data set.

domain.description: This is the domain that the token will be limited to.

user-scope.description: This is the scope that will be be used to enforce permissions for this API key and access.

ip-address.description: Optional IP restrictions for API to require access from matching IP or subnets. Multipe ips/subnets can be provided comma seperated

readonly.description: If enabled it will limit any get Scope to a read only verion providing only GET acces to the read actions. POST, PUT and DELETE will be rejected.

can-create-keys.description: If enabled it will allow this key to make keys for lesser scopes then itself. It will not allow creations of scopes same or higher access level.

anyOf.description: A string with a specific model or "*" for all.

items.description: A string with a specific model or "*" for all. The specific models reference the API's scope defintions stored in the DB.

anyOf.description: An array of models.

allowed-models.description: This is an optional limitation to the ability of this apikey. The default is  * or all models, but can be set to a specific model or array of models allowing specific access. The "models" are linked to objects as defined in the scope definition and might have varies terminolofy from the api itself.

created-datetime.description: The datetime that the key was created.

lastused-datetime.description: The datetime of the last time the apikey was used for API access. The timestamp is acurrate up to the hour to avoid overhead in tracking high volume requests

reseller.description: The name of the Reseller. This is the main ID for the group which will be used to link domains for ownership.

description.description: This description can be used to give a more complete description and notes for this reseller.

domains-total.description: This is the total number of domains in this Reseller's account.

users-total.description: This is the total number of users in all domains owned by this reseller.

email-send-smtp-hostname.description: This is the SMTP server hostname

email-send-smtp-port.description: This is the SMTP port for sending emails.

email-send-smtp-username.description: This is the username used in connecting to SMTP  server for sending email.

smtp_pwd.description: This is the password used in connecting to SMTP  server for sending email.

active-calls-against-license-count.description: This is the current active calls counting against the license including on net calls.

active-calls-offnet-current.description: This is the current active calls across all domains that are calls coming from or going to offnet numbers.

emergency-address-id.description: This is an autogenerated ID for the Address.

domain.description: This is the domain that the token will be limited to.

address-name.description: This is the name label for the address

caller-name.description: This is the caller's name for the address

address-line-1.description: This is the address line 1 for the address

address-line-2.description: This is the address line 2 for the address

address-city.description: This is the city for the address

address-state-province-abbreviation.description: This is the state or province (Canadian) abbreviation for the address

address-postal-code.description: This is the postal code for the address

address-country-abbreviation.description: This is the country appreviation for the address

address-location-description.description: This is an optional location description for the address

ip-address-public.description: This is the public IP of the client window creating the address.

address-formatted-pidflo.description: This is a return value of the formatted PIDFLO standardized value for the address

carrier.description: This is the carrier name for the address

count-users-configured.description: This is the count of how many users are using the particular address

address-formatted-pretty.description: This is a human readable format of the address

address-is-default-for-site.description: This is whether the address is a default for the site

time-frame.description: This is the main timeframe name that will be used as the name and will be used in conjuction with answer rules.

domain.description: This is the domain that the token will be limited to.

time-range-data.description: This is an array of time range objects that will make up the timeframe.

enabled.description: This will enable IP checking. Default is no

parameters.description: This field will provide the values needed to configure the parent feature. These values could be multiple strings or numbers.

domain.description: This is the domain that the token will be limited to.

time-frame.description: This is the timeframe that also is needed to make this set of answer rules unique. * is teh default and if no timeframes are setup this is is how to set anserrules.

enabled.description: This allows any added configurations to specify a hostname. Default to "yes"

is-active.description: This is read only option that will be true if this timeframe is active for this user when taking into account current settings, date/time and all timeframes.

order.description: This is to allow for a priority based order of different timeframes. The default is to add it in value higher (lower priority) than the lowest priority timeframe. Timeframe * will get 99 if not already set.

simultaneous-ring.description: This is a feature that will allow multiple phones or remote extensions to be called at the same time.

enabled.description: This will enable IP checking. Default is no

parameters.description: This field will provide the values needed to configure the parent feature. These values could be multiple strings or numbers.

do-not-disturb.description: When enabled this feature will block all inbound ringing and send the call to voicemails if enabled.

enabled.description: This will enable IP checking. Default is no

call-screening.description: When enabled this feature will enable the call screening application asking the caller to record their name before attempting the connection based on other answer rules.

enabled.description: This will enable IP checking. Default is no

enumDescriptions.top: When inputting "top", this will move the answerrule to the first place in the priority list of answerrules
enumDescriptions.bottom: When inputting "bottom", this will move the answerrule to the last place in the priority list of answerrules

new-position.description: This will move the answerrule to the top or bottom in the priority list. Top having the most priority, and bottom having the least priority

unique-id.description: This is a unique id that will be generated automatically on new contact create.

phonenumber.description: This is the main phonenumber setting. Should be a  formated in a e164 format with country count and number. US domestic numbers should be similiar to 18005551234

enabled.description: This allows any added configurations to specify a hostname. Default to "yes"

domain.description: This is the domain that the token will be limited to.

dial-rule-parameter.description: This is the application parameter field that can be used for additional settings based on the dial-rule-application. Default is empty string.

dial-rule-translation-destination-user.description: This is the forward destination the call will be sent to in partnership with the application (dial-rule-application) and host (dial-rule-translation-destination-host). This might be a user, queue or offnet number depending on the application.

dial-rule-translation-destination-host.description: This is the forward destination host the call will be sent to in partnership with the application (dial-rule-application) and user (dial-rule-translation-destination-user). This might be the domain name or a connection name if trying to route offnet.

dial-rule-translation-source-name.description: This field will allow customization of the source "name" often used for teh caller id name or the the descriptive quoted value outside of the sip uri. Defaults to [*] but you can add a prefix for example with something like this, sales_[*]

dial-rule-description.description: This is a description of the number/rule that can be used for defintion or tracking purposes.

domain.description: This is the domain that the token will be limited to.

dial-rule-dial-plan.description: This is the dial plan name the dial rule exists in.

dail-rule-matching-to-uri.description: This is the main digit string match for the dial translation.

dialrule.description: This is the dialrule id that will be given with each read action that will be needed to read back individual value, update specific rules or delete rules. If wanting to programatically generate the dial rule id is a base64 encoding of the concatination with "||" of the following fields in order, dail-rule-matching-to-uri, dail-rule-matching-from-uri, dail-rule-matching-day-of-week, dail-rule-matching-start-date, dail-rule-matching-end-date, dail-rule-matching-start-time, dail-rule-matching-end-time

enabled.description: This allows any added configurations to specify a hostname. Default to "yes"

dail-rule-matching-from-uri.description: This is a source match that would allow for a specific dial rule match based on the caller as well as the forward destination of dail-rule-matching-to-uri.

dial-rule-matching-day-of-week.description: This is a field that will control the DOW (day of week) match for this rule. Default is * or to match all the time, but valid format will allow any single day of the week 0-6 (sunday-saturday) or a range in square brackets like [0-6]

dial-rule-matching-start-date.description: This is the start of a the valid date range for this rule.  Keep to * if you do not want to use date range for this rule.

dial-rule-matching-end-date.description: This is the end of a the valid date range for this rule.  Keep to * if you do not want to use date range for this rule.

dial-rule-matching-start-time.description: This is the start of a the valid time range for this rule.  Keep to * if you do not want to use time range for this rule. The format is a valid HH:MM format from 00:00 to 23:59. The timezone would be the user's timezone if passing through a user already.

dial-rule-matching-end-time.description: This is the end of a the valid time range for this rule.  Keep to * if you do not want to use time range for this rule. The format is a valid HH:MM format from 00:00 to 23:59. The timezone would be the user's timezone if passing through a user already.

dial-rule-parameter.description: This is the application parameter field that can be used for additional settings based on the dial-rule-application. Default is empty string.

dial-rule-translation-destination-scheme.description: This setting controls the "sip schema" on the destination, in most all case setting to "[*]" is correct.

dial-rule-translation-destination-user.description: This is the forward destination the call will be sent to in partnership with the application (dial-rule-application) and host (dial-rule-translation-destination-host). This might be a user, queue or offnet number depending on the application.

dial-rule-translation-destination-host.description: This is the forward destination host the call will be sent to in partnership with the application (dial-rule-application) and user (dial-rule-translation-destination-user). This might be the domain name or a connection name if trying to route offnet.

dial-rule-translation-source-name.description: This field will allow customization of the source "name" often used for teh caller id name or the the descriptive quoted value outside of the sip uri. Defaults to [*] but you can add a prefix for example with something like this, sales_[*]

dial-rule-translation-source-scheme.description: This setting controls the "sip schema" on the destination, in most all case setting to "[*]" is correct.

dial-rule-translation-source-user.description: This field will allow customization of the source "user" which is often the caller id number or the value before the @ in then SIP uri. Defaults to [*] but you can modify and include [*] as the previous value. An example would be using a token like <OwnCidNmbr> to replace with a user's caller id number.

dial-rule-translation-source-host.description: This field will allow customization of the source "host" which is  the value after the @ in then SIP uri. Defaults to [*] but you can modify and include [*] as the previous value. An example would be using a token like <OwnDomain> to replace with a user's domain

dial-rule-description.description: This is a description of the number/rule that can be used for defintion or tracking purposes.

code.description: This is the HTTP error code that was returned.

message.description: This is a more detailed description of what the error was and any potential correction steps.

call-id.description: This will be the callid for the requested call. This should be a random string generated by your application and stored if you want to read back the call later or end it.

dial-rule-application.description: The application can be used to help with next destination selection.

call-term-user.description: This will be the destination or termination number for the call request. This will be used once the call is connected to the orgination number.

call-orig-user.description: This field will allow you to select the device, user or number the first leg of the call request will connect to. If left blank the origination will be the user@domain of the user making the request.

auto-answer-enabled.description: If enabled it will request auto answer headers for the first leg (origination) of the call.

caller-id-number.description: This setting will allow for the callerid of the termination leg to be set, by default it will be the preconfigured callerid of the user making the request.

callback-caller-id-number.description: This is the callerid that will be used when calling the first leg(callback) of the call request. If left empty it will default to the user's caller id.

call-term-added-sip-header.description: This field allows for a added sip header on the destination leg of the call.

grant_type.description: This is the type of authentication request being made. 'password' is most common if using username and password from end user to grant access.

client_id.description: This is the client_id provided to the developer for accessing the api. Will match with the client_secret

client_secret.description: This is the client_secret provided to the developer for accessing the api. Will match with the client_id

username.description: This is the username and logic for the user. This may look like a uid (user@domain) or could be something else if setup diffferent including a email address potentially.

password.description: This is the password for the user requesting access.

login-username.description: This is the login for the user used to access the portals and other applications. The default will be [user]@[domain] if not set otherwise.  This value is required to be unique across the entire system so 409 will be returned if not unique.

time-zone.description: The timezone which the meeting was created with

dial-plan.description: This is the dial plan set for the user. Dial plans are used to control action and forward options that will be used when dialing out from the user.

dial-policy.description: This value is the dial policy/permission for this user whch will control the destinations this user can dial. Generally dial policys are preconfigured at system level and selected at user level.

voicemail-user-control-enabled.description: This allows any added configurations to specify a hostname. Default to "yes"

phone-numbers-to-allow-enabled.description: This allows any added configurations to specify a hostname. Default to "yes"

phone-numbers-to-reject-enabled.description: This allows any added configurations to specify a hostname. Default to "yes"

call-screening-enabled.description: This allows any added configurations to specify a hostname. Default to "yes"

language-token.description: This is the language that is set for the user to be used for portal viewing.

limits-max-data-storage-kilobytes.description: This is the maximum value in kilobytes that is allowed to be stored in the user's voicemail, audio greetings and fax storage.

limits-max-active-calls-total.description: This is the maximum number of active calls allowed for the user.

directory-annouce-in-dial-by-name-enable.description: This allows any added configurations to specify a hostname. Default to "yes"

voicemail-enabled.description: This allows any added configurations to specify a hostname. Default to "yes"

voicemail-receive-broadcast-enabled.description: This allows any added configurations to specify a hostname. Default to "yes"

reject-anonymous-calls-enabled.description: This will enable IP checking. Default is no

voicemail-playback-announce-datetime-rec.description: This will enable IP checking. Default is no

voicemail-playback-announce-caller-id.description: This will enable IP checking. Default is no

voicemail-playback-sort-newest-to-oldest.description: This allows any added configurations to specify a hostname. Default to "yes"

caller-id-number.description: This is the main caller id number for the user to be used for outbound calls outside of the domain.

caller-id-name.description: This is the main caller id name for the user to be used for outbound calls outside of the domain.

caller-id-number-emergency.description: This is the main caller id that will be used for emergency calls when placed by the user.

area-code.description: This is a area code for the user. I will be used when user dial's 7 digits to translate it to full 10 or 11 digit number.

directory-name-visible-in-list-enabled.description: This allows any added configurations to specify a hostname. Default to "yes"

emergency-address-id.description: Value will need to be an exact match to an address-id which links to a address for DLR (dynamic location routing) for emergency calling

music-on-hold-randomized-enabled.description: This will enable IP checking. Default is no

active-calls-total-current.description: This read only setting is the current active call count for this user.

created-datetime.description: This datetime is when the user was created.

last-modified-datetime.description: This datetime is when the user was last modified.

callqueue-agent-id.description: This is the main identifier for a "agent" in a queue. There are two format supported here, either a user based option which will look like user@domain or a device based method like sip:device@domain.

callqueue.description: This is the callqueue id for which the agent is in. The agent may be in multiple callqueus, but this value is a single entry.

domain.description: This is the domain that the token will be limited to.

callqueue-agent-availability-type.description: This is the main toggle for the agents status in the queue.  "automatic" is logged in, which manual is logged out with the option for one call. From a angent perspective and the actions of logging in and out the recomention would be to use the specific "agent actions" api instead of the basic agent update request.

callqueue-agent-wrap-up-allowance-second.description: This is the number of seconds that the system will hold the agent offline before logging them back in automatically. This time allows the agent to comolete needed notes and conclusions to previous calls and prepare for next call.

auto-answer-enabled.description: This will enable IP checking. Default is no

callqueue-agent-dispatch-order-ordinal.description: For use in callqueues that have a linear order, this value would be to the order of the agent in that list, the lower the number the higher agent in the order.

callqueue-agent-dispatch-queue-priority-.description: This setting is a priority value for this agent's entry with respect to the other callqueues they are in. Lower number is higher priority. This means that when the agent comes back available after a call the priority would be used to determine which queue its attempted first.

limits-max-active-calls-total.description: This is the maximum number of calls an agent will be given at at time. Default and ideal is 1, but if needed it can be higher

callqueue-agent-answer-confirmation-enab.description: This will enable IP checking. Default is no

callqueue-agent-has-registered-devices.description: This read only status will show the agents registration infor and if the system believes the device is registered.

callqueue-agent-availability-for-dispatc.description: This is the status for the agent generated by the system using all available informaion.

active-calls-total-current.description: This is the total number of calls on the agent's account across all their queues

last-modified-datetime.description: This timestamp is the datetime of the last change to the agent's configuration and status

callqueue-agent-max-concurrent-sms-conve.description: This is the maximum number of SMS queue sessions the agent will be requested to handle before queing up future requests.

last-callqueue-dispatch-datetime.description: This timestamp is the datetime of the last dispatch to the agent.

callqueue-agent-entry-type.description: This is a read only field that will tell you the type of agent config that is setup, this might include device or user based agents or "offnet"

login-username.description: This value read only from the user that is linked to this agent. Its the user's login if needed.

name-full-name.description: This value read only from the user that is linked to this agent. Its the user's full name.

agent-name-first-name.description: This value read only from the user that is linked to this agent. Its the user's first name.

agent-name-last-name.description: This value read only from the user that is linked to this agent. Its the user's last name.

agent-department.description: This value read only from the user that is linked to this agent. Its the user's depeartment.

agent-site.description: This value read only from the user that is linked to this agent. Its the user's site

agent-user-scope.description: This value read only from the user that is linked to this agent. Its the user's scope.

agent-status-message.description: This value read only from the user that is linked to this agent. Its the user's status message.

callqueue.description: This is the main call queue extension which is the identifier for this call quieue.

domain.description: This is the domain that the token will be limited to.

description.description: This is a simple description of the callqueue which can be used to better explain the use or purpose of this call queue.

callqueue-dispatch-type.description:
This is the main dispatch type for hunting agents. The options are described below. 
#Round-robin
#Tiered Round-robin
#Ring All
#Linear Cascade
#Linear Hunt
#Call Park

callqueue-calculate-statistics.description: Enabled will trigger music on hold for the domain and allow it to be customized.

active-queued-calls-total-current.description: This is the current calls waiting in the call queue waiting to be dispatched.

callqueue-agent-dispatch-timeout-seconds.description: This is the timeout in seconds the call queue will attempt an agent before timing out and retrying additional resources.

callqueue-count-agents-available.description: This is the current number of agents logged in and available for work.

callqueue-count-agents-total.description: This is the current number of agents configured for this callqueue.

callqueue-debug.description: Enabled will trigger music on hold for the domain and allow it to be customized.

callqueue-force-full-intro-playback.description: Enabled will trigger music on hold for the domain and allow it to be customized.

callqueue-max-callback-queueing-hours.description: This setting will allow callback's in the callqueue allowing callers to press a buton and opt for a callback instead of waiting. 0 the default is off, any other positive number will enable callback and allow callback up to that many hours after the callback is requested.

callqueue-max-current-callers-to-accept-.description: This setting is the maximum number of callers allowed in the queue before forward busy answer rules will be followed. 0 is unlimited

callqueue-max-current-wait-to-accept-new.description: This setting is the maximum expected wait time  allowed in the queue before forward busy answer rules will be followed. 0 is unlimited

callqueue-max-wait-timeout-minutes.description: This is the max wait time in minutes that it would allow a call to wait before being forced out of the queue. 0 is unlimited

callqueue-require-available-agents-to-ac.description: This will enable IP checking. Default is no

callqueue-source-match.description: This is a optional setting that will control the source match for the forward dispatch leg of calls allowing if needed different matches to connecting devices when calls come through the queue.

callqueue-sms-enable.description: This will enable IP checking. Default is no

callqueue-sms-phrase-initiate-chat.description: This is the keyword that will be the trigger for a new SMS queueing session

callqueue-sms-phrase-initiate-success.description: The message to auto reply to a inbound SMS message once the trigger keyword is detected

callqueue-sms-phrase-initiation-fail.description: The message that will be auto replied when a message wihtout the keywork is detected.

callqueue-sms-phrase-no-agents-available.description: The message that will be auto replied when there are no agents available and the system is unable to setup a sms chat session at this time.

callqueue-sms-phrase-terminate-chat.description: This is the keyword that will be the trigger to end a active SMS session.

callqueue-sms-phrase-terminate-success.description: The message that will be played after a successfully termination of a session via a keyword.

callqueue-agent-auto-logout-after-missed.description: This will enable IP checking. Default is no

callqueue-sim-ring-1st-round.description: When using a callqueue-dispatch-type value that allows for mutlple agents to be run on at a time this setting controls the amount of agents that are dispatched in the first round.

callqueue-sim-ring-increment.description: When using a callqueue-dispatch-type value that allows for mutlple agents to be run on at a time this setting controls the amount of agents that will be added to the group activly ringing at each agent timeout level.

device.description: This is the "device" id which will be the first part of the device-sip-registration-uri which can be used to read specific device information.

domain.description: This is the domain that the token will be limited to.

device-sip-registration-uri.description: This read-only value is the full SIP uri and will often be sip:[device]@[domain]

device-sip-registration-state.description: This is the current registration status for the device. This value will be registered if its in the value time windoe

device-sip-registration-password.description: This is the password which would be used for device registration. If left unset on device create it will create a new random password with a default length of 16 characters.

caller-id-number-emergency.description: This is a override value for emergency caller id value that would be specific to this device and would override the user's set value.

device-force-notify-new-voicemails-enabl.description: This will enable IP checking. Default is no

device-level-call-recording-enabled.description: This will enable IP checking. Default is no

device-push-enabled.description: This allows any added configurations to specify a hostname. Default to "yes"

device-sip-registration-contact.description: This is the contact value for the device from the last successfull registration.

device-sip-registration-core-server.description: This is the hostname for which the last registration was completed.  This would be used to connect the device from on inbound call.

device-sip-registration-datetime.description: This date-time will be the last registration time of the device. This time may be slightly delayed to improve replication throughput.

device-sip-registration-expires-datetime.description: This date-time will be the time at which the device will be treated as unregisted and should be in the future for active registed devices.

device-sip-registration-expiry-seconds.description: This is the requested re-register perior for the device. The device will be treated as unregistered if they do not register in this window + a system wide grace period.

device-sip-registration-ignore-for-prese.description: This will enable IP checking. Default is no

device-sip-registration-ignore-report-en.description: This will enable IP checking. Default is no

device-sip-registration-ip-address.description: This is the read-only value is the IP:port for the last successfull registration.

device-sip-registration-user-agent.description: This is the read-only value for the user-agent used during the last successfully registration.

device-sip-no-to-tag-in-cancel.description: This will enable IP checking. Default is no

device-srtp-enabled.description: This setting will enable SRTP (secure/encrypted audio) on the the device. Yes will force and "opp

auto-answer-enabled.description: This will enable IP checking. Default is no

device-sip-allowed-user-agent.description: This value can be used to restrict registration to a specific User Agent or partial match of User Agent.  This allows for an extra layer of secutiry if you know the expected user agent.

device-sip-nat-traversal-enabled.description: This is the main nat transveral setting. In most all device cases "automatic" is the correct answe

device-sip-latency-seconds-current.description: This is the latency from the latest registration measured in seconds from the time the core sent a 407 challenge to the 200ok with Auth.

device-sip-latency-seconds-average.description: This is the average of the last 5 registration attempts measured similiarly to device-sip-latency-seconds-current.

emergency-address-id.description: This value if set will be the address-id specific to this device for emegeny calling

error-reading-from-endpoint-module.description: If yes, it means the API had an issue accessing the NDP for mac specific data

login-username.description: This is a read-only value of the user's login for the owner of the device.

name-full-name.description: This is a read-only value of the user's full name for the owner of the device.

device-models-model.description: This is an optional model that when provided with device-provisioning-mac-address will link this device to the mac address in the device provisioning platform.

device-provisioning-mac-address.description: This is the mac address that has this device linked to it. This field can be used to link a mac address on device create. Must also provide a device-models-model

device-provisioning-registration-core-se.description: This is the SiPbx Servers primary server config from a list of valid

device-provisioning-sip-transport-protoc.description: This is the transport that will be requested when provising the device.

device-provisioning-username.description: The provisioning username for the request to authenticate the configuration request.

device-provisioning-password.description: The provisioning password for the request to authenticate the configuration request.

device-provisioning-line.description: Optional setting to select which line is used on the device when provisionined with device-models-model and device-provisioning-mac-address

device-provisioning-overrides.description: This is the overrides set for the mac address in the provisioning server on the mac linked to this device.

domain.description: This is the main name of the domain. This name should be capable of being in SIP enpoints so no special characters or spaces

territory.description: This is the Reseller name field. All domain's should be limied to a reseller.

description.description: Description of domain. Can be any plain text summary of the domain or organization.

rmoh.description: Enablement of Random Music on Hold by default

email_sender.description: This e-mail address will be displayed on e-mails sent from the system

language.description: The default language for the domain
